The Turton similarity has come up already in this training, but in a different context. Earlier we pointed out that similarity is an activator of the principle of liking and refers to commonalities of tastes and preferences and experiences. However, unity-based similarity is a different concept. It refers to commonalities of membership in we groups that share of personal or social identity. Unity-based similarity is not so much about a feeling of being like others as it is about a feeling of belonging with others who share an important personal or social identity, of being not just like them, but of them, of one of them.
